title: License-snapshot capture for referenced data sources
context: The cancer-data guardrail requires provenance on every referenced data source including a captured copy of the license text or page. Without an automated snapshot tool, this step is error-prone: reviewers may accept a dead link or an undated page that has since changed terms. A committed snapshot (license text + SHA-256 hash + Wayback save URL) makes license verification auditable and durable. Depends on gate-002, where the provenance model is defined.
objective: Build and commit a Elyos-side helper tool that, for any referenced data source, captures a committed copy of the license text/page, computes its SHA-256 hash, saves it to the Wayback Machine, and records the path + hash + timestamp in exampleData.provenanceRef.
acceptanceCriteria: For any referenced data source, the tool captures a committed copy of the license text/page plus its SHA-256 hash plus a Wayback Machine save URL.
The exampleData.provenanceRef field records the committed path + hash + capture timestamp.
Code is MIT-licensed; pnpm test and CI are green; no credentials embedded.
The tool is usable by triage-009 and subsequent contribution tasks for license-snapshot capture.
output: A committed MIT-licensed Elyos-side helper tool for license-snapshot capture, with tests green and CI passing, enabling auditable provenance on all referenced data sources from M1 onward.
